---
title: |
  <img src="../_assets/ahri_logo.png" alt="AHRI logo" class="title-logo" />
  <br />
  The Developmental Cascade of Social Determinants and Behavioral Phenotypes on Adolescent Depression
subtitle: "A Longitudinal Causal Mediation Framework"
author:
  - name: CO-LUMINATE DSU Team
    affiliation: Africa Health Research Institute (AHRI)
    city: Durban & Somokhele
    country: South Africa
format:
  revealjs:
    controls: true
    progress: true
    slide-number: true
    zoom: true
    css:
      - styles/presentation_0.css
      - styles/presentation_1.css
      - styles/presentation_obj04.css
---

## Motivation

- CO-LUMINATE links harmonized youth mental-health data to AHRI HDSS social-exposure histories.
- Linked analysis cohort: 5,279 adolescents and young people aged 13-24 years.
- SAP focus: quantify longitudinal social-determinant effects on depression and mediation through behavioral pathways.

## SAP-Aligned Aim and Questions

- Aim: assess the longitudinal impact of social determinants on youth depression using a causal mediation framework.
- Research question 4.1: how are social determinants associated longitudinally with depression among young people?
- Research question 4.2: what are the causal pathways between low SES, crowding, caregiver co-residency, and depression?
- Research question 4.3: are these relationships mediated by migration, substance use, and sexual behavior?

## CO-LUMINATE Linked Data Sources {.smaller}

| Study | Design | Youth sample (13-24) | Study period | MH timepoints | Tool(s) |
|---|---|---:|---|---:|---|
| DREAMS | Observational cohort | 2,184 | 2017-2019 | 3 | SSQ-14 |
| Multilevel | Observational cohort | 1,833 | 2018-2019 | 2 | SSQ-14 |
| Isisekelo Sempilo | Individually randomized trial | 859 | 2020-2021 | 1 | SSQ-14, PHQ-9 |
| Treatment as Prevention (TasP) | Cluster-randomized trial | 403 | 2012-2016 | 6 | PHQ-9 |

- Total linked sample in SAP: 5,279.

## Developmental Cascade Perspective

![](/_assets/Longitudinal%20Cascade.png){width=90%}

- Time-ordered modeling links life-course household exposures to later behavioral and depression outcomes.

## Causal Mediation Structure {.dag-zoom-slide}

<a href="/_assets/DAG-01.svg" target="_blank" class="dag-zoom-link" title="Open zoomable DAG in a new tab">
  <img src="/_assets/DAG-01.svg" alt="Causal mediation DAG" />
</a>
<p class="dag-zoom-note">Click the DAG to open the full-size SVG in a new tab (browser zoom supported).</p>

## DAG Components

- Exposure domain: low SES, crowding, caregiver co-residency (longitudinal HDSS).
- Mediator domain (SAP, individual-level): migration, substance use, and sexual behaviour.
- Outcome domain: depression derived from harmonized SSQ-14/PHQ-9 framework.

## SAP Mediators (Explicit)

- Migration
- Substance use
- Sexual behaviour
- These mediators are tested as behavioural pathways between social determinants and depression.

## Youth Co-Creation Input to the SAP

- Youth Advisory Group (YAG) develops a lay DAG from photo-diary and narrative sessions.
- Co-created DAG is refined with researchers and fed into the causal analysis plan.
- YAG interpretation workshop is used to contextualize and ground-truth findings.

## SAP Analysis Plan: A to D

1. A: describe social-determinant trajectories using longitudinal latent class analysis.
2. B: estimate interactions between determinants and mediators (migration, substance use, sexual behaviour) with mixed-effects regression.
3. C: evaluate SSQ-14 against PHQ-9, derive age-specific cutoffs (13-19, 20-24), estimate prevalence by wave.
4. D: fit confounder-adjusted logistic models, then estimate causal effects using DAG-guided g-computation.

## Causal Estimation Strategy (SAP Step D)

- Use adjusted logistic models to identify social factors associated with depression.
- Include associated social factors as causal exposures in the mediation framework.
- Use g-computation to estimate effects under time-varying exposures.
- Estimate mediation by behavioral factors at individual level (migration, substance use, sexual behavior).

## Planned Outputs and Translation

- Longitudinal linked dataset and reproducible analytic workflow.
- Pathway-specific evidence on direct and mediated social-determinant effects on depression.
- Policy-relevant signals for social protection, crowding-responsive housing interventions, and caregiver-support programming.
- Scalable method for SAPRIN and APCC network contexts.

## Thank You

- CO-LUMINATE DSU Team
- Africa Health Research Institute (AHRI)
- SAP content source: `_assets/proposal/proposal_content.md` (Sections 4.2 and 5.2.2).
